mnemonics.title: Trophic Levels Order
mnemonics.content: PPCS - Producers → Primary → Secondary → Tertiary Consumers. Remember: 'Panda Eats Bamboo Carefully' (P→E→B→C)

mnemonics.title: Abiotic Factors
mnemonics.content: STOWAS: Sunlight, Temperature, Oxygen, Water, Air ( gases), Soil (pH, minerals)

mnemonics.title: 10% Energy Law
mnemonics.content: TEN PERCENT: Transfer Energy, Not Perfectly Every Natural Organic Tissue Uses Neatly - only 10% moves up!

acronyms.expansion: Trophic levels, Abiotic factors, Nutrition cycle, Sunlight source, Energy flow, Levels of consumers, Your ecosystem knowledge
acronyms.usage: Remember key aspects of ecosystem structure for exams

acronyms.expansion: Photosynthesis, Renewable energy (solar), Output glucose, Decomposers depend, Useful biomass, Consumers feed, Energy base, Recycles nutrients, Sun-dependent
acronyms.usage: Remember characteristics of producers in ecosystem

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a downward arrow: Sun → Producers → Primary Consumers → Secondary Consumers → Tertiary Consumers. Think of it like stairs going down with each step losing energy.
Imagine a real pyramid with widest base (producers - 10,000 J) narrowing to top (apex predators - 10 J). Each level is 10% of the level below.
BIOtic = LIVING (BIO means life). ABIOTIC = NON-LIVING (A means 'without' - without life). Use 'A' for 'Alone/non-living'.
Draw a circle/cycle: Soil → Plants → Animals → Decomposers → Soil. Like a recycling loop that never ends, unlike energy which flows one way.

analogies.analogy: Think of an ecosystem like a factory: Producers are the raw material suppliers, consumers are the workers using materials, decomposers are the cleanup crew - all essential for the factory to function.
Like a web/net where pulling one string affects multiple others. If one species is removed, many others are impacted - just like removing a person from a social network affects many connections.
Energy flow is like money spent once - you earn it, spend it, and it's gone. Nutrient cycling is like recycling - you use it, collect it back, and use it again.
Like a restaurant hierarchy: Owner (sun) → Chefs (producers) → Waiters (primary consumers) → Managers (secondary) → Customers (territory). Each level serves the one above but gets smaller.
